title: Agents and Infrastructure, Sam Lambert | Compile 26
summary: Sam Lambert, CEO of PlanetScale, argues that database infrastructure must be redesigned for agent-driven workflows. The talk covers agent-optimized database operations including sharding, branching, deploys, safety mechanisms, and the importance of human-first infrastructure design to enable safe autonomous optimization loops.
writeup: PlanetScale CEO Sam Lambert argues that database infrastructure needs to be redesigned for agent-driven workflows rather than retrofitted onto tooling built for humans alone. The talk covers agent-optimized database operations including sharding, branching, deploys, and safety mechanisms, and it frames human-first infrastructure design as the basis for safe autonomous optimization loops. The takeaway: as agents take on database work, branching, versioning, and built-in safety testing become the guardrails that let optimization run without losing control.
